# Squint

> Squint (https://squint.mdzabeeh.com) is a free, browser-based arcade of
> five-minute games that train a designer's instincts: visual accuracy,
> letter spacing (kerning), colour perception and divergent thinking.
> Tagline: "Train the eye you trust." No account needed; camera-based games
> run entirely on-device and never upload video.

## Games

- [Eyeball It](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/eyeball-it/): bisect, centre,
  judge angles and proportions by eye; scored on pixel error with
  design-spec redline feedback and micro-lessons (e.g. optical vs
  geometric centre).
- [Kern Combat](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/kern-combat/): drag letters to
  even spacing, graded against the typeface's own kerning metrics; teaches
  the optical rules of letter spacing.
- [Colour Forge](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/colour-forge/): mix HSL colour
  to match targets (including from memory and by complement); scored with
  the perceptual CIEDE2000 formula plus a per-channel breakdown.
- [Thirty Circles](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/thirty-circles/): the classic
  Bob McKim / IDEO divergent-thinking sprint — thirty circles into thirty
  different things in three minutes; drawn by webcam-tracked hand or mouse;
  reflection on fluency, flexibility, originality.
- [Hand Tetris](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/tetris/): gesture-controlled
  Tetris via on-device MediaPipe hand tracking.
- [Daily Warm-Up](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/warm-up/): one round of each
  game chained into a five-minute daily ritual with streak tracking.

## How it teaches

Every round ends with a teach-back reveal (the correct answer overlaid on
the player's attempt, the error measured, and the design principle behind
the miss), progress is tracked locally with accuracy trends, and round
selection adapts toward the player's weakest challenge types.

## More

- [About & FAQ](https://squint.mdzabeeh.com/about/): what Squint is, who
  it's for, privacy details, and how scoring works.
